item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Our Lady Of The Eternal Shark Week

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novoting, because I feel like this climax doesn't quite live up to the amount of build-up we get. The MTF log is not nearly as intense as I anticipate it to be, so that's anticlimactic. The logs could be condensed a little, but I don't find them to be too boring. The imagery is pretty strong, though, so that almost makes up for its other issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: mostly in agreement with roget on this one - i really liked the beginning and ending (save for the redaction, which i'll get to in a moment) but everything between that was a slog. you could condense the letters into one or two longer ones, and i don't really feel that the apocrypha contributes anything of narrative value to the reader at all. the cssmith in me also wishes you'd used a better background for the letters, it's a bit hard to read crossed with the cursive font. I will say though that the ending log was very tight, very gross and fitting. that said, the redaction is very plainly purely for narrative purposes and has no real in-universe sense. that's not to say that it needs one necessarily, it just feels a bit paper-thin to the reader when its clearly for dramatic effect. overall though, good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The historical fiction here is a lot of fun, although I feel like the logs in the middle of the article go on for a bit longer than they needed to. I also found it distracting that a French aristocrat would be handwriting in English, wouldn't it make more sense for the handwritten sections to be in French and have English translations?

After the strong start, by the end of the four logs and the bit about the Watcher I was growing less and less interested, and the ending didn't do much for me at all. This lacks the character and fun that made 3862 work as well as it did.

I think if you consolidated the information presented in the notes to be maybe one long letter, with an English translation to French text, along with further consolidation of the last three log sections to keep the nice bits of visceral description for the watcher, the action of the log and the bubbling soup of the ending, it might be enough to carry a whole article. As it stands now, there's too much filler and not enough character for this to stand on its own feet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forgive me mother sarkic, for I have sinned.

Lovely article, has some great imagery and deeper ideas hidden within it.

+1

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
"Your sexy sweet solution is prime for distribution."
Sorry, not sorry.

Thanks to the following critters, please Wikidot PM me if you want crediting:

  • MalyceGraves
  • Dr Moned
  • SharpEmbrace
+ Spoilers
"There's something strange afloat in the sewers"
